title: Break-Even Point — Units & Dollars
event: Reyes Co. has fixed costs of $120,000, a $20 contribution margin per unit, and a 40% contribution margin ratio. Compute the break-even point in units and in sales dollars.

computationPaths.narration: Break-even asks: how many $20 contributions does it take to bury $120,000 of fixed costs?

memorize.body: BE units = Fixed costs ÷ CM per unit. BE dollars = Fixed costs ÷ CM ratio.
Divide by CM, never by price — dividing 120,000 by the $50 price gives a meaningless 2,400.

questions.prompt: Fixed costs $120,000, CM per unit $20. Break-even in units = ?

distractors.feedback: You divided by the $50 PRICE. Only the $20 contribution margin pays fixed costs: 6,000 units.
You divided by the $30 variable cost. The denominator is CM (price − VC) = $20.
